data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

The fix is to include the generated file in `Compile` items within the target:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, so the file is generated before compilation, but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in the compilation because it's not added to the `Compile` items.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target, after the file is generated.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in the compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This is a common pitfall with MSBuild code generation — the file must be explicitly added to the `Compile` item group within the same target that creates it, since the default glob evaluation happens before custom targets run.
